perturbation.original_text: This promotion shall begin on June 1,1999 and shall terminate June 1, 2000 (herein "Term") This term shall be extended for a 1 year period provided 3000 pagers per month are distributed to Purchase customers.
perturbation.changed_text: This promotion shall begin on June 1,1999 and shall terminate June 1, 2000 (herein "Term") This term may be extended based on future negotiations between both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original contract specifies that the Term is extended automatically if 3000 pagers per month are distributed. By changing it to 'may be extended based on future negotiations', it creates an internal contradiction because it no longer provides the condition that warrants that automatic extension, thus affecting sections where the 'Term' is referenced assuming an automatic extension. This contradiction will affect the enforcement of the agreement, as it is no longer clear under which exact circumstances the promotion will be extended.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. Go Call shall prepare and distribute at its own expense, all advertising materials to be used for this promotion. b. Go Call, shall submit in advance, all artwork and advertising to PageMaster Corporation for approval as provided in Paragraph 8. c. Go Call shall not engage in the same or similar promotions during the Term of this Agreement with any other entity providing paging services, equipment or other related products and services.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear condition for the automatic extension of the 'Term'. The changed text removes this condition and replaces it with a negotiation-based extension, which contradicts the original agreement and creates uncertainty about when the agreement will be active, thus causing a contradiction with the contradicted section, which includes the line 'Go Call shall not engage in the same or similar promotions during the Term of this Agreement' as it is no longer clear for how long the term will be, or under what conditions it will be extended, causing a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the execution of this Agreement, Go Call shall forward to PageMaster Corporation a deposit in the sum of $100,000.00 to secure the availability of 100,000 pagers to all Purchase Customers who prepay their annual airtime for this promotion. The deposit is non-refundable except as follows: a. PageMaster Corporation shall refund to Go Call, $1.00 per pager on all pagers delivered to Purchase Customers pursuant to this promotion (net return) up to the maximum refund of $100,000.00. b. On the last day of each month, the refund of Go Call's portion of the deposit shall be calculated by PageMaster Corporation for the &bbsp; prior month and will be forwarded to Go Call by check, along with an extended accounting of all pagers and customers until September 1, 2000, unless otherwise instructed by Go Call. Go Call, upon ten (10) days written notice, shall have the right to examine the books and records of PageMaster Corporation to verify the sales resulting from this promotion. Such examination shall be made at the regular place of business of PageMaster Corporation where such books and records are maintained during normal business hours and shall be conducted at Go Call's expense by a certified public accountant or other Go Call executive so designated by Go Call.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the execution of this Agreement, Go Call shall forward to PageMaster Corporation a deposit to secure the availability of pagers to all Purchase Customers who prepay their annual airtime for this promotion. The deposit is non-refundable except as follows: a. PageMaster Corporation shall refund to Go Call based on pager delivery. b. On the last day of each month, the refund of Go Call's portion of the deposit shall be calculated by PageMaster Corporation for the &bbsp; prior month and will be forwarded to Go Call by check, along with an extended accounting of all pagers and customers until September 1, 2000, unless otherwise instructed by Go Call. Go Call, upon ten (10) days written notice, shall have the right to examine the books and records of PageMaster Corporation to verify the sales resulting from this promotion. Such examination shall be made at the regular place of business of PageMaster Corporation where such books and records are maintained during normal business hours and shall be conducted at Go Call's expense by a certified public accountant or other Go Call executive so designated by Go Call.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific deposit amount of '$100,000.00 to secure the availability of 100,000 pagers' and the specific refund amount of '$1.00 per pager', the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the financial obligations. It states a deposit is needed to secure pagers, but the exact amount isn't present. Likewise, it only mentions that a refund is given 'based on pager delivery', not the exact amount. These omissions contradict the original intent to define financial terms, leading to disagreements on financial liabilities if disputes arise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PageMaster Corporations' liability shall in no event exceed an amount equivalent to the amounts received by PageMaster Corporation hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the deposit amount as $100,000 and the refund amount as $1 per pager. The changed text removes these specific amounts, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text, 'PageMaster Corporations' liability shall in no event exceed an amount equivalent to the amounts received by PageMaster Corporation hereunder,' relies on a clearly defined 'amount received'. By removing the specified deposit amount, it is no longer clear what the 'amount received' is, thus contradicting the liability clause. This fits the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that preparation for and execution of the promotion necessitates the exchange of confidential and proprietary information relating and belonging to the parties to this Agreement, as well as to other third parties integral to the promotion, including, without limitation, the pager manufacturer and the airtime supplier (herein "Information"). Each party agrees (1) to review, examine, inspect, obtain or utilize the information only for the purpose of this promotion, (2) to otherwise hold such Information strictly confidential, (3) to prevent the disclosure of such Information to nonessential third parties without a "need to know", and (4) to insure that each party's employees, agents and representatives and those of any integral third party understand and are bound by the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement. Each party shall indemnify the other party with respect to any loss or damage arising from the unauthorized disclosure or use of the Information by their respective employees, agents and representatives, or by those of any third party to whom such Information was disclosed. The agreements contained in this Paragraph shall survive the expiration, or termination of this Agreement. The panics hereby agree that subsequent to the expiration or termination of this Agreement, each party consents to the other party's use of its name only in connection with advertising to their respective trade or industry.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that preparation for and execution of the promotion necessitates the exchange of confidential and proprietary information relating and belonging to the parties to this Agreement (herein "Information"). Each party agrees (1) to review, examine, inspect, obtain or utilize the information only for the purpose of this promotion, (2) to otherwise hold such Information strictly confidential, (3) to prevent the disclosure of such Information to nonessential third parties without a "need to know", and (4) to insure that each party's employees, agents and representatives understand and are bound by the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement. Each party shall indemnify the other party with respect to any loss or damage arising from the unauthorized disclosure or use of the Information by their respective employees, agents and representatives, or by those of any third party to whom such Information was disclosed. The agreements contained in this Paragraph shall survive the expiration, or termination of this Agreement. The panics hereby agree that subsequent to the expiration or termination of this Agreement, each party consents to the other party's use of its name only in connection with advertising to their respective trade or industry.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'as well as to other third parties integral to the promotion, including, without limitation, the pager manufacturer and the airtime supplier', the scope of 'Information' is significantly narrowed. The original text explicitly includes information related to these third parties as confidential. The change now makes it uncertain if information from/about these third parties are covered under confidentiality, which contradicts the intent to protect all confidential data related to the promotion. This creates problems for section 9 and the indemnification section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall indemnify and hold harmless the other from any loss or damages, including reasonable attorneys' fees incurred by the other because of claims, suits or demands based on personal injury, death or property damage or third party claims, suits or demands of any kind to the extent such loss or damage is caused by or results from the negligent or willful acts or omissions of the other or its employees or agents, including but not limited to the unauthorized use of the trademark, logos, or other property of third parties without the consent and approval of PageMaster Corporation. PageMaster Corporation's participation in the promotion does not constitute an endorsement of the products or services of Go Call nor does Go Call's participation in the promotion constitute an endorsement of PageMaster Corporations or any third party's products or services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit inclusion of third parties (pager manufacturer and airtime supplier) in the definition of 'Information'. The contradicted text refers to 'third party claims, suits or demands of any kind'. By narrowing the definition of 'Information', it creates a contradiction regarding whether claims arising from unauthorized use of information related to those previously mentioned third parties (pager manufacturer, airtime supplier) are covered under the indemnification clause, making the indemnification obligation unclear and potentially unenforceable, which contradicts the original intent.
